figures_detected.description: Exploded/assembly diagram showing main bearing layout: thrust bearing (upper only), upper bearing, lower bearing, oil grooves, No.1 bearing (flywheel side), front orientation
Top view of cylinder block showing main bearing cap bolt tightening sequence numbers (1 through 10)
Photo showing crankshaft being rotated by hand after bearing cap bolts are secured, verifying smooth rotation
Photo showing dial gauge setup to measure crankshaft end play

steps.text: Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap.
notes: Confirm that correct main bearings are used. Refer to "Inspection" (EM-5040).
No. 2 and No. 4 main bearing halfshells have an oil groove.
The oil grooves of the thrust bearing should face the crankshaft.

steps.text: Install crankshaft and main bearing caps and tighten bolts as follows: Main bearing cap bolts — (1) Tighten to 20 N·m (2.0 kg-m, 14.8 ft-lb) in order shown. (2) Turn bolts to 50° degrees clockwise with an angle wrench in order shown.
notes: After securing bearing cap bolts, make sure crankshaft turns smoothly by hand.
If beyond the standard clearance, replace thrust bearing with a new one.

items.condition: Angle tighten clockwise with angle wrench in order shown

tools_referenced.name: Angle wrench
tools_referenced.usage: Turn main bearing cap bolts 50° clockwise in Step 2
